# Sudo NOPASSWD Misconfiguration

**Platform:** linux · **Category:** misconfiguration · **Severity:** high

A sudoers NOPASSWD rule on a shell-escapable binary lets a low-privilege user reach a root shell without ever knowing the root password.

**MITRE ATT&CK:** [T1548.003 — Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Sudo and Sudo Caching](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1548/003/)
## Description

`sudoers` can grant a user the ability to run specific binaries as root without a password (`NOPASSWD`). If any of those binaries can spawn a shell, read arbitrary files, or execute attacker-supplied code — editors, pagers, interpreters, and dozens of others catalogued by GTFOBins — the intended restriction ("only run this one binary") collapses into full root access. The vulnerability isn't sudo itself; it's a mismatch between "this binary looks safe to whitelist" and "this binary can be made to do anything."

## Requirements

- A sudoers entry granting NOPASSWD on a binary with a known shell-escape (e.g. `dan ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/vim`)
- The binary itself installed and reachable via $PATH
## Exploitation

1. Run `sudo -l` to enumerate NOPASSWD-permitted commands for the current user.
2. Cross-reference each command against a shell-escape catalogue (GTFOBins).
3. Invoke the escape, e.g. `sudo vim -c ':!bash'`, to obtain a root shell.
## Proof of Concept

- [`poc.sh`](./poc.sh)
## Detection

| Type | Artifact | Description |
|------|----------|--------------|
| osquery | [`detections/osquery/linux_sudo_nopasswd.conf`](./detections/osquery/linux_sudo_nopasswd.conf) | Periodically flags sudoers entries granting NOPASSWD on ALL commands. |
| sigma | [`detections/sigma/linux_sudo_nopasswd.yml`](./detections/sigma/linux_sudo_nopasswd.yml) | Flags process creation for sudo invocations of known shell-escapable binaries. |

## Mitigation

- Never grant NOPASSWD on interpreters, editors, or pagers; scope sudoers rules to a single fully-qualified binary and, where possible, fixed arguments.
- Use `sudoedit` instead of granting sudo on an editor directly.
- Alert on `sudo -l` reconnaissance and on sudo invocations of binaries listed in GTFOBins.
- Run periodic sudoers audits (e.g. the osquery check below) rather than relying on one-time review at grant time.
## References

- [GTFOBins](https://gtfobins.github.io/)
- [MITRE ATT&CK T1548.003](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1548/003/)
